response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine geospatial domain exposure through founding GISCollective and years of GIS platform work, plus proven low-latency systems experience (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second on Linux). However, his professional C++ experience is brief and dated (a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015), he lists no Rust, and his recent work has centered on TypeScript/Node.js product engineering rather than systems programming. He has no direct routing, tiling, or navigation guidance experience, so while he could plausibly grow into the role, he does not meet the core C++/navigation requirements today.
strengths: Real geospatial data experience: founded and maintains GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map
Low-latency distributed systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at scale of millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and comfort learning across the full stack
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No routing, map-tiling, or navigation guidance systems background
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node.js web product work, a significant distance from systems-level pipeline engineering

key_factors.factor: Low-latency distributed systems experience on Linux
